summary_one_line: Input must carry the IPv6 next-header byte equal to kProtoIcmp6 (0x3a) so the switch dispatches to the ICMP6 case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An IPv6 packet whose next-header / protocol field byte equals the ICMPv6 protocol constant kProtoIcmp6 = 0x3a, so that the `switch (aIpProto)` at ip6.cpp:938 matches `case kProtoIcmp6` and routes to mIcmp.HandleMessage at line 972.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry the literal 0x3a (ascii ':') in the parsed protocol position — seed 1 has `10 3a` at offset 0x06-0x07 and the byte-diff shows offset 0x29 concentrated on 3a(:)x8 for W versus 00 for L; these match the kProtoIcmp6 dispatch value, so the W role uniquely hits line 972 `case kProtoIcmp6` (W marked, L not).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds never settle the protocol field on 0x3a — at the divergent offset 0x29 the L bytes are 00(.)x7, never the 3a needed, so the loser falls through to the default arm and blocks the true side; the per-trial vector shows naive resolved only 2/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry recorded when `aIpProto` is compared against the immediate kProtoIcmp6 (0x3a) in the switch lowering, then substitutes 0x3a into the input at the offset whose old byte matched the other CMP operand — directly planting the magic protocol constant that naive's blind havoc mutators must stumble onto by chance.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the kProtoIcmp6 arm of a switch on aIpProto at ip6.cpp:938, true side globally blocked.
Taking the case routes to mIcmp.HandleMessage at line 972, hit only by the winner role.
Winner cmplog seed 1 carries 0x3a in the parsed protocol position.
At divergent offset 0x29 the W bytes concentrate on 3a while L bytes are 00.
Naive loser resolved only 2/10 while cmplog winner resolved 10/10.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S, only havoc + token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13326 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 4f1af37e9d5867a4` and observed the I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 25 of the 26-level chain, which in cmplog/vpc indicates an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on `if (proto_byte == 0x3a)` where cmplog and naive resolve at statistically equal rates — i.e. naive plants 0x3a as fast as cmplog despite no CMP introspection.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset that maps to aIpProto is inferred from byte-diff concentration (0x29 / 0x07) rather than a confirmed parse offset, so the 0x3a placement could be at a different protocol field than assumed.
